narrative:
It doesn't get better than today. After that chilly start earlier, we're in for about the nicest June day imaginable across the mountains — comfortable, sunny, and refreshingly dry. The yearning to get outdoors is a severe productivity threat affecting millions across the Southern Appalachians today. Tonight turns cool again in valleys. Thursday warms up but stays pleasant. By Friday, humidity creeps up, bringing the potential for widely scattered afternoon thunderstorms. Shower and thunderstorm potential lingers through the weekend.

This week's Monday Message is now available for our subscribers. Severe weather potential for Monday, along with a return to classical summertime weather by next weekend are topics of discussion.

2027 Blue Ridge Calendar Pre-Publication Sale is Underway!

The 2027 Blue Ridge Calendar is being printed now and will be available around July 1. Until then, the Pre-Publication Sale is underway with the best price we will offer all year. Get your 2027 calendars at 26% off when you buy 2 or more. As in previous years, we have scheduled special Pickup times around NW NC to save you the cost of shipping. Click here for pickup times and locations.

You can see all the winning photography at our Photo Contest page .

Forecast Discussion

Yesterday's cold front is well south, and surface high pressure now builds in overhead. That high is the whole story today — sinking air, low humidity, and sunshine aside from some high clouds. The dry air warms efficiently, so despite a chilly morning, temperatures recover nicely this afternoon.

The high holds tonight — another cool night in valleys. Thursday it slides off the coast, and return flow nudges humidity back in. Warmer but pleasant; can't rule out a stray afternoon shower on the NC side.

Friday the humidity increases. No front — just enough heat and moisture for widely scattered afternoon thunderstorms. That repeats through the weekend with the possible need to dodge a thunderstorm each afternoon. Saturday night, the potential for showers and thunderstorms remains overnight, especially on the west side of the Appalachians.

By Tuesday, the warm ridge strengthens in the Southeast, turning up the heat. Storm chances drop to isolated, but anything that fires can pack a punch.

Detailed forecast discussion and analysis is only available to RaysWeather supporters.

Latest Insights
Monday Message for June 22, 2026

The week starts stormy, but ends up like the first full week of summer should, with hit-or-miss afternoon thundershowers and very warm temperatures. It's the Monday Message, available for our subscribers.

Jun 22, 2026
Yadkin Valley Deluge

A morning complex of thunderstorms moving across the Ohio Valley and into West Virginia and Virginia produced an outflow boundary that pushed southward into North Carolina during the afternoon of June 18. This outflow boundary, the result of a cool rush of air from thunderstorms occurring to the north, served as an effective focusing mechanism for training thunderstorms that occurred over parts of Surry, northern Wilkes and southern Alleghany County during Thursday afternoon and evening.

This occurred under the backdrop of increasing tropical moisture returning from the southwest as the remnants of Tropical Storm Arthur moved into Alabama.

The result was excessive rainfall, in the range of 2-4 inches, with locally higher amounts of around 5 inches in central Surry County.
